plannerRationale.agencyDesign: Two required baseline nodes establish evidence for both source groups before offering route choice. Route A (Spell Check) provides audio-prompted spelling construction for silent-letter targets that need deeper encoding. Route B (Word Radar visible read) reinforces high-frequency sight vocabulary with fast recognition repetition. Both routes feed into Mystery for preference evidence, then locked Quest targeting silent-letter transfer, then locked Boss for final mastery gate.
plannerRationale.evidenceDesign: Baseline 1 measures silent-letter spelling construction using partial visual recall to capture per-word accuracy and letter-order errors. Baseline 2 measures high-frequency word pronunciation/recognition using visible read mode because the source purpose is recognize, not spell_from_memory. Route activities extend baseline evidence with varied instruments: Spell Check adds audio-prompted retrieval for silent letters; Word Radar visible read adds fast recognition flow for high-frequency words. Mystery collects preference without claiming mastery. Quest and Boss remain locked until baseline and quest evidence justify unlocking.
plannerRationale.layoutChoice: Used boardTemplate slots 1-8 with horizontal-adventure-spine preset. Start at slot 1, two baseline nodes at slots 2-3, choice gate at slot 4, route nodes at 5a.1 (upper) and 5b.1 (lower), Mystery at slot 6, Quest at slot 7, Boss at slot 8. All node thumbnails selected from boardTemplate.art.nodeThumbnails. Background image and palette used exactly as specified in boardTemplate.

lock.reason: Complete baseline and route practice to unlock the Quest challenge.
lock.label: Locked

lock.reason: Complete Quest evidence to unlock the final Boss challenge.
lock.label: Locked

options.label: Spell Check Silent
options.description: Practice spelling silent-letter words with audio prompts

choiceSignal.expectedEvidence: Preference for audio-prompted spelling construction over visual recognition flow

options.label: Fast Recognition
options.description: Read high-frequency words quickly for fluency

choiceSignal.expectedEvidence: Preference for fast visible-read recognition over audio-prompted spelling

evidenceUsed.summary: Benchmark Advance Unit 9 Week 3 with two labeled groups: Silent Letters (10 words) and High-Frequency Words (10 words)
Board generated from stored source assignment and local OCR/text extraction.

openQuestions: Which silent-letter words will Reina miss on first attempt?
Does Reina already recognize all high-frequency words, or will some require pronunciation support?
Will route choice preference align with skill confidence, or will Reina choose variety?
What level of audio-prompt support will silent-letter targets require before independent recall?

planTheory.hypothesis: Reina's silent-letter spelling depends on explicit orthographic memory for kn-, wr-, -mb, and gn- patterns, while her high-frequency word reading depends on whole-word visual recognition. The two groups require different measurement instruments: spelling construction for silent letters (because the teacher heading and pattern focus signal production goals) and visible-read pronunciation for high-frequency words (because the teacher heading signals recognition/fluency goals, not spelling production).
evidenceSummary: Source document separates two labeled groups with different teacher-assigned purposes
Silent Letters group contains pattern-focused words (kn-, wr-, -mb, gn-) requiring orthographic production memory
High-Frequency Words group contains Benchmark Advance sight vocabulary for reading fluency, not spelling drill
No prior session evidence exists for Reina; this is the first baseline session
masteryContext shows 2 days until test with urgent intensity, requiring fast baseline evidence and efficient coverage
planTheory.intervention: Measure silent-letter spelling using Word Radar partial visual recall (letter-by-letter construction with hidden word) to capture per-word accuracy and error patterns. Measure high-frequency word recognition using Pronunciation visible read mode to capture fluency and decoding, not spelling production. Offer route choice after both baselines: Route A extends silent-letter evidence with Spell Check audio-prompted spelling; Route B extends high-frequency evidence with Word Radar visible-read flow. Mystery provides preference evidence without contaminating mastery. Quest and Boss remain locked until baseline and quest evidence justify unlocking.
supportCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows ≥7/10 correct first try, suggesting pattern is accessible and route practice can deepen encoding
High-frequency baseline shows ≥8/10 correct pronunciation, suggesting recognition is strong and visible-read flow can build automaticity
Route choice shows clear preference and completion, confirming agency model fits Reina's engagement style
Mystery completion with positive affect confirms reward timing and type are appropriate
reviseCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows 3-6/10 correct or multiple retries, indicating pattern needs explicit teaching before more construction drills
High-frequency baseline shows 4-7/10 correct or hesitation, indicating recognition needs more visible-read repetition before fluency demands
Route choice shows skip or abandonment, suggesting route options need revision or stamina is lower than expected
Mystery shows neutral or skip, indicating reward type or timing mismatch
falsifyCriteria: Silent-letter baseline shows ≤2/10 correct or refusal, indicating silent-letter pattern is not accessible and construction drills are premature
High-frequency baseline shows ≤3/10 correct or decoding struggle, indicating sight vocabulary is not yet automatic and needs direct teaching
Route choice shows refusal or frustration, indicating agency model is wrong for Reina or baseline work was too hard
Mystery shows negative affect or early exit, indicating reward is aversive or mistimed

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: ≥7 words correct first try with low latency and no help requests confirms baseline competence
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 3-6 words correct or multiple retries/help requests indicates need for audio-prompted support and pattern teaching
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: ≤2 words correct or refusal/frustration indicates silent-letter pattern is not yet accessible and needs explicit teaching before more drills

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: ≥8 words pronounced correctly first try with fluency confirms recognition baseline
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: 4-7 words correct or hesitation/skips indicates need for visible-read repetition and fluency support
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: ≤3 words correct or decoding struggle indicates high-frequency words are not yet automatic and need direct teaching

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Child chooses and completes chosen route with engagement and low frustration
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Child skips or abandons chosen route, suggesting route mismatch or fatigue
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Child refuses choice or shows frustration at gate, indicating agency model needs revision

plannedMeasurements.supportCriteria: Child engages with mystery content and completes with positive affect
plannedMeasurements.reviseCriteria: Child skips or shows neutral engagement, suggesting reward type mismatch
plannedMeasurements.falsifyCriteria: Child refuses or shows negative affect, indicating reward timing or type needs revision

Route B (Word Radar visible read) reinforces high-frequency sight vocabulary with fast recognition repetition.
